Title: Why Patagonia? — Edward Champion
Description: I feel awkward about my body, I don't feel like the picture of an outdoorsy person, and I work indoors for a living. Why do I buy Patagonia clothes? A mix of quality, warranty, and the company's commitment to its products. I like a little ethics in my clothes.

Title: Does using LLMs make me dumber? - Wilsons Blog
Description: So this is something I’ve heard a lot and have thought about a lot. First I want to re-frame the question from what I usually read to what I think is actually a good question: “How does using LLMs change the way I learn?” I think this is better because 1. the dumb-intelligent axis is […]

Title: Understanding KV Cache: The Hidden Memory Cost of Serving LLMs
Description: A deep dive into KV cache — the hidden memory bottleneck of LLM inference. Learn how KV cache grows with context length and concurrency, why it matters for self-hosted models, and how attention innovations like GQA, MQA, MLA, sliding-window attention, hybrid architectures, quantization, PagedAttention, and prefix caching reduce memory pressure.

Title: Human Bottlenecks
Description: Why AI won't augment human capabilities much.

Title: Highest Random Weight in Elixir | jola.dev
Description: A description of HRW/rendezvous hashing and the HRW elixir library.

Title: Intelligence
Description: Some problems don’t get solved in your head, they get solved in someone else’s while you watch or read their solution. You might feel it before you understand it. A small calibration. A quiet “Oh!”

Title: Containers Are a Security Boundary (some assembly required) ┈ Tianon's Ramblings ✿
Description: I've heard "containers are not a security boundary" enough times that it's started to feel like received wisdom, and my honest read (after 13+ years) is that it's technically defensible but practically sloppy – and the sloppiness matters.

Title: Adults Have Lost the Ability to Smile, Why?
Description: Today at the supermarket, I was smiling at a baby. The baby saw my smile and she smiled back. I did a few more times with older kids and they all smiled back...

Title: Lessons I Learned from Creating Searx
Description: Seven years of maintaining a popular open source metasearch engine taught me a great deal about privacy, community, and the fundamental limits of the metasearch approach. Here is what led me to build Hister.

Title: Back up your TOTP codes by just printing them

Title: Google's Antigravity Bait and Switch
Description: The day was to begin like any other, with Antigravity open, expecting to get some work done before my attention fragments. But Google had other plans. They had rolled out a new version of Antigravity the day before, at I/O 2026, presenting it as a shiny, standalone Codex-style experience.
